headline: Micron surges 16% on blowout earnings
detail: Micron's blockbuster print signals strong semiconductor demand, which is a direct positive read-through for your chip names. Watch for SMH and AVGO to get a lift at the open — that could be your cleanest window to trim AVGO at a better price than yesterday. Don't mistake one stock's pop for a trend change in your losing positions.

headline: Iran warns ships over Strait of Hormuz
detail: Iran threatening to control one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints is a real geopolitical risk. An energy price spike from any escalation would fan inflation fears and pressure the Fed's rate path — bad for growth and tech multiples, which is exactly what your book is concentrated in. Worth keeping an eye on.

headline: Wall Street roster-cut day Friday — massive volume incoming
detail: Friday is shaping up to be one of the highest-volume days of the year due to options expiration and institutional rebalancing. High volume days can cause sharp, fast moves in either direction — on margin, that's extra exposure. Know your stops cold before Friday.

headline: Anthropic hiring spree for AI data centers
detail: Continued AI infrastructure buildout by major players like Anthropic keeps the demand narrative alive for chips and cloud — a tailwind for names like AVGO and PLTR. It doesn't fix your current loss percentages, but it confirms the sector thesis isn't broken, just extended and volatile right now.
